doc_meta.title: Programmable Python Script Comparison + Analyzer System
doc_meta.primary_goal: Deterministically compare and analyze Python scripts to detect redundancy, measure similarity, extract component signatures, and produce evidence suitable for rule-based decisions and canonical selection.

core_philosophy.deterministic_layer: Produces evidence and enforces hard stops for state changes (e.g., quarantine eligibility). No heuristic output may override this layer.
core_philosophy.heuristic_layer: Produces hypotheses and ranking signals to reduce human review load; must be convertible into requests for deterministic evidence; cannot directly mark code obsolete or force quarantine.

source_files.role_in_system: Defines the governing separation of deterministic vs heuristic layers, and enumerates safe heuristic capabilities (scouting signals) that must feed deterministic evidence collection.
key_takeaways_used: Deterministic layer decides state, enforces hard stops, controls quarantine eligibility; cannot be overridden.
Heuristic layer generates hypotheses and ranking; cannot force quarantine or override reachability; must drive evidence collection.

source_files.role_in_system: Defines the critical gap (Python Introspection Layer) and the success criteria required to unlock rule execution and canonical selection.
key_takeaways_used: Introspection must support structural + semantic similarity, dependency overlap, I/O surface detection, capability fingerprinting, and an orchestration comparison engine.
Must feed evidence paths required by rules.

source_files.role_in_system: Rule configuration that consumes analyzer evidence (similarity scores, dependency overlap, I/O surface, reachability, etc.) to produce decisions (ACTIVE, OBSOLETE_CANDIDATE, DUPLICATE_SET_CANDIDATE) and rankings (CANONICAL_PREFERENCE).
key_takeaways_used: Similarity thresholds for redundancy detection and gating.
Weighted canonical preference scoring formula with disqualifying gates (syntax/lint failures).
Broader rule-sets that require analyzer evidence channels (dependency hygiene, dead code, safety harness, etc.).

source_files.role_in_system: Research-derived deterministic rubric patterns for selecting a canonical file among duplicates (multi-signal scoring + deterministic tie-breakers) and for producing auditable JSONL outputs.
key_takeaways_used: Multi-signal scoring (e.g., recency, completeness, naming intent, validity) with deterministic tie-breakers.
Strict audit logging outputs (JSONL + trace logs) and self-healing iteration caps (bounded).

source_files.role_in_system: Specification for AST-based structural similarity measurement and acceptance criteria.
Specification for semantic similarity measurement (behavioral intent approximation) and acceptance criteria.
Specification for extracting import dependencies and computing dependency overlap for similarity and impact analysis.
Specification for extracting I/O surface signatures (args/env/files/network) and computing overlap for redundancy confirmation and behavior-delta estimation.
Orchestration spec for comparing two files (or sets), combining analyzers, producing a unified similarity report, and supporting downstream ranking/decisions.

must_do: Compute structural similarity (AST-based) and semantic similarity scores for Python files.
Extract dependency profiles and compute overlap (e.g., Jaccard).
Extract I/O surface profiles and compute overlap.
Extract component signatures and stable component IDs suitable for registry/evidence linkage.
Produce an evidence bundle that can feed rule evaluation (including redundancy detection and canonical preference ranking).
Generate deterministic, auditable outputs (JSON/JSONL) with reproducible scoring and tie-breakers.
must_not_do: Heuristic outputs must not directly change state (e.g., cannot quarantine/retire code by themselves).
Never emit non-reproducible scores without pinned versions, fixed preprocessing, and explicit config.

top_level_pipeline.name: Ingest + Identity
determinism_controls: stable file identifiers (absolute_path hash + size + mtime snapshot)

top_level_pipeline.name: Normalization
determinism_controls: normalize encoding to UTF-8 with strict error policy
canonicalize indentation only for analysis (never rewrite source)

top_level_pipeline.name: Parsing + Indexing
preferred_parsers: tree-sitter-python (optional for error-tolerant parsing; must pin grammar version)

methods: function/class signature sketches (names optional; arity and decorator sets preferred)
embedding of normalized code (with comments/docstrings policy explicit)
embedding of extracted API surface summary (exports + docstrings)
optional: embedding of CFG/callgraph summary if available
extract import statements (stdlib vs third-party vs local)
extract env var reads (os.environ / getenv patterns)
extract file read/write paths (static patterns + pathlib/open usage)
domain tags and risk/security tags as evidence channels
stable component IDs: hash of normalized AST subtree + signature sketch
component signature record: inputs/outputs (where extractable), deps, io surface touches

forbidden_adjustments: changing thresholds without recording config diff
state changes based on heuristic-only signals

required_contract: Heuristic signals are stored as ranking-only fields.
Triage engine consumes rule decisions; heuristics only influence what evidence to collect next.

sqlite_index.purpose: Fast lookup + caching of derived analyzers keyed by sha256; supports incremental runs.
tables: files(file_id, path, sha256, size, mtime, parse_status)
pair_scores(file_a, file_b, structural, semantic, dep_jaccard, io_overlap, composite)

jsonl_outputs.purpose: Append-only auditable logs for downstream tooling and evidence bundles.

commands.name: index
commands.does: Build/refresh the SQLite index, parse files, and compute per-file evidence.

commands.name: compare
commands.does: Run all analyzers and emit a ComparisonReport.

commands.name: find-duplicates
commands.does: Find duplicate sets using rule thresholds + composite similarity.

commands.name: rank-canonical
commands.does: Apply canonical preference scoring for each duplicate set.

details: Map analyzer outputs into evidence paths required by DOC-CONFIG-PY-IDENTIFY-SOLUTION-001__MAPP_py_identify_soultion.yml
Trace logs for every decision and tie-breaker
Bounded reflection loop (max 3) for resolving missing deterministic evidence

success_criteria: Produces structural + semantic similarity scores, dependency overlap, and I/O overlap deterministically for any parseable Python file.
Can identify duplicate sets and produce canonical rankings that satisfy configured thresholds and disqualifiers.
Outputs are reproducible: same inputs + same toolchain + same config => identical results.
Heuristic signals never directly trigger state changes; they only prioritize evidence collection and ranking.
